project.name: hermes-war-room
project.description: A browser dashboard on top of Hermes Agent multi-profile delegation and kanban systems, making agent fleets visible, legible, and directable from a single screen. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Frontend UI
layers.description: Nuxt/Vue application shell, route pages, reusable workstation/mission/team components, and visual presentation assets.

layers.name: Frontend State, Composables, and Types
layers.description: Client-side Composition API state, polling/SSE composables, markdown/token helpers, avatar utilities, and shared TypeScript data shapes used by the Vue interface.

layers.name: Server API Routes
layers.description: Nitro API endpoint handlers that expose configuration, profiles, tools, skills, models, usage, kanban tasks, and mission orchestration operations to the browser UI.

layers.name: Server Orchestration Services
layers.description: Server-side utilities and plugins that integrate with Hermes/Kanban, maintain mission state, roster metadata, profile configuration, task feeds, auto-nudges, and ACP/orchestrator execution.

layers.name: Project Configuration, Documentation, and Automation
layers.description: Root manifests, Nuxt/ESLint/release configuration, documentation, CI/CD workflows, runtime environment hints, and other repository-level automation or policy files.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and package manifest to understand that War Room is a Nuxt dashboard for making Hermes multi-profile delegation and Kanban work visible and directable.
tour.languageLesson: Nuxt projects combine Vue UI files with server-side Nitro routes, so understanding both app/ and server/ is essential.

tour.title: Application Shell and Main Screens
tour.description: Inspect the app shell and route pages that frame the War Room, Missions, and Team views before diving into lower-level components.

tour.title: Mission Control UI Components
tour.description: Follow the mission and workstation components that render live delegation state, task cards, operative drill-downs, and command surfaces in the browser.

tour.title: Client-Side State and Live Streams
tour.description: Review the composables that poll Kanban state, subscribe to mission streams, transform task feeds, render markdown, and keep the UI reactive.
tour.languageLesson: Vue Composition API composables centralize reusable reactive behavior; files named useX typically expose shared stateful logic.

tour.title: Browser-to-Server API Boundary
tour.description: Trace the Nitro API routes that the UI calls to create missions, dispatch Kanban work, list profiles and capabilities, and fetch runtime state.

tour.title: Mission and Orchestration Core
tour.description: Dive into the server utilities that persist mission state, emit live updates, run Hermes turns, handle locks, decompose work, and integrate with ACP/orchestrator execution.

tour.title: Profiles, Roster, Skills, and Tooling
tour.description: Inspect the support utilities that discover Hermes profiles, manage roster metadata, read/write profile configuration, and surface skills, tools, MCP servers, presets, and agent metadata.

tour.title: Automation and Release Surface
tour.description: Finish with repository-level automation: CI, release configuration, lint/typecheck setup, and workspace/runtime metadata that support building and shipping the dashboard.
